content: Language learning vocabulary acquisition (the linguistic educational context of the word "reflexion") can serve as a blueprint for improving LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as learners internalize the meaning of "reflexion" by repeatedly encountering it across different contextual uses (the educational definition found in Topic A) and reflecting on how its nuance shifts across contexts, LLMs can be trained to refine self-reflection by iteratively grounding their reflection in diverse task-specific observed outcomes, analogous to how humans learn the concept of reflection itself. This creates a recursive loop where the learned concept of reflection improves the agent's capacity to reflect.
